top_signals.title: Super typhoon Bavi heading to Taiwan & China might disrupt commodity supply chain and Pacific vessel supply
top_signals.summary: Super Typhoon Bavi is currently rated Category 5 by ECMWF, and Windy tracking shows it heading toward Taiwan and China. It is expected to reach the coast on Friday, 10th July, with wind gusts of up to 100 knots. However, by Friday 10th July, Bavi could cross one of the busiest shipping lanes in the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Eight Japanese ships depart Hormuz via passage near Iran
top_signals.summary: Investing.com — At least eight vessels controlled by Japan’s Mitsui OSK Lines Ltd. are leaving the Strait of Hormuz through a passage near Iran, including some of the final trapped oil tankers to depart the waterway. The ships consist of five supertankers, each able to transport 2 million barrels of crude, two chemical tankers and ...

top_signals.title: LNG shipping stocks: The decline continues, but at a slower pace
top_signals.summary: The UP World LNG Shipping Index lost 3.14 points (1.59%) last week, closing at 194.60 points, while the S&P 500 gained 1.76% in a holiday-shortened week. The UPI continues to weaken following a slowdown in its decline, though the weighted index lost only a quarter of a per cent. The ratio of advancing to declining ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: SEC plans crypto rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers in 2026 regulatory agenda
top_signals.summary: The SEC is laying the groundwork for cryptocurrency rule changes before the end of the year, with a blueprint for exchanges and brokers.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Summer lull deepens across coaster markets despite the start of the new grain season
top_signals.summary: A gradual increase in small-tonnage cargo offer has come to a halt in the Black Sea. “It seems that after several weeks of pre-season activity and more enquiries from grain traders, we are seeing another uneventful week with even fewer fresh offers,” a Ukrainian broker commented to ISM. Meanwhile, Mediterranean cargo flows are steadily dull ...
